nodes.name: TV Show Settings
nodes.description: Where famous television shows are set

items.prompt: In what city is the NBC sitcom (1989–1998) created by Larry David and Jerry Seinfeld—often called 'a show about nothing'—primarily set?
items.explanation:
Set on the Upper West Side of Manhattan, centered on Jerry's apartment at 129 West 81st Street
Exterior shots used a real building on West 81st Street near Columbus Avenue
Tom's Restaurant on Broadway at 112th Street served as the exterior of Monk's Café, the gang's regular diner
Filmed mostly at CBS Studio Center in Studio City, California—not in New York
The show's New York feel came from stock footage, establishing shots, and writing steeped in city life
Co-creator Larry David later based Curb Your Enthusiasm in Los Angeles
Popularized distinctly New York concepts: "close talkers," subway etiquette, apartment envy

items.prompt: In what Southwestern U.S. city is the AMC drama (2008–2013) about a high school chemistry teacher turned methamphetamine manufacturer primarily set?
items.explanation:
Both set and filmed on location in Albuquerque, New Mexico—one of TV's most authentic location shoots
Creator Vince Gilligan originally envisioned Riverside, California, but New Mexico's tax incentives shifted production
The desert landscape became central to the show's visual identity—vast, sun-bleached, isolating
Real Albuquerque landmarks appear throughout: Dog House Drive In, Twisters (as Los Pollos Hermanos), the car wash on Menaul Blvd
Walter White's house at 3828 Piermont Dr NE became a tourist attraction; owners installed a fence to stop pizza-throwing fans
Spawned prequel Better Call Saul (2015–2022), also set and filmed in Albuquerque
Boosted Albuquerque tourism significantly—the city now offers official Breaking Bad tours

items.prompt: In what city is the NBC sitcom (1982–1993) set in a bar 'where everybody knows your name,' starring Ted Danson as a former baseball player turned bartender?
items.explanation:
Set in a basement bar in the Beacon Hill neighborhood of Boston
The exterior was the real Bull & Finch Pub at 84 Beacon Street, across from the Boston Public Garden
Bull & Finch accepted just one dollar for rights to use its exterior; it renamed itself "Cheers" in 2002
Interior scenes filmed entirely at Paramount Studios in Los Angeles—the real pub looks nothing like the TV set
Sam Malone (Ted Danson) was a former Red Sox relief pitcher, grounding the show in Boston sports culture
Ran 11 seasons and 275 episodes; spawned the spinoff Frasier (1993–2004)
The pub became one of Boston's top three tourist destinations by 1990

items.prompt: In what Pacific Northwest city does the NBC sitcom (1993–2004) about a radio psychiatrist who moves home after his divorce take place?
items.explanation:
Spinoff of Cheers—Frasier Crane moves from Boston to Seattle after his marriage ends
Frasier's luxury apartment is in the fictional Elliott Bay Towers, with a panoramic view of the Space Needle
The apartment's view was created from a vantage point where no real building exists
Frasier hosts a call-in radio show on KACL 780 AM, a fictional Seattle station
Filmed almost entirely in Los Angeles; only one episode (the 100th) was shot on location in Seattle
The show's Seattle identity came through constant references to coffee culture, rain, and the city skyline
Revived in 2023 on Paramount+ with Kelsey Grammer, this time set in Boston

items.prompt: In what East Coast port city is the HBO drama (2002–2008) created by former journalist David Simon, examining institutions from police to drug trade to schools, set?
items.explanation:
Both set and filmed on location in Baltimore, Maryland
Creator David Simon was a former Baltimore Sun reporter; co-creator Ed Burns was a former Baltimore homicide detective
Each season focused on a different city institution: street-level drug trade, port workers, city hall, public schools, the media
Used real Baltimore neighborhoods: West Baltimore's housing projects, Fells Point, the Inner Harbor, the Sun newsroom
Cast included many non-actors from Baltimore, including former drug dealers and police officers
Often cited as the most realistic portrayal of an American city ever put on television
Barack Obama called it his favorite TV show; it is frequently assigned in university sociology and urban studies courses

items.prompt: In what U.S. state is the HBO drama (1999–2007) about a mob boss who sees a therapist while managing his crime family and domestic life set?
items.explanation:
Set in northern New Jersey, primarily in the affluent suburbs of Essex and Bergen counties
Tony Soprano's house was a real home at 14 Aspen Drive in North Caldwell, New Jersey
The opening credits show Tony driving from New York City through the Lincoln Tunnel into New Jersey on the Turnpike
Bada Bing! strip club exterior was a real club called Satin Dolls on Route 17 in Lodi, NJ
Filmed extensively on location throughout northern New Jersey and parts of New York City
The show deliberately avoided the usual TV depiction of the Mafia as a glamorous New York phenomenon
New Jersey's unglamorous suburban sprawl—strip malls, diners, parking lots—became the show's distinctive visual signature

items.prompt: In what English county is the ITV period drama (2010–2015) about an aristocratic family and their servants set, spanning the early 20th century from the Edwardian era through the 1920s?
items.explanation:
Set at the fictional Downton Abbey estate in Yorkshire, England, from 1912 through the late 1920s
The exterior and most interiors were filmed at Highclere Castle in Hampshire—not actually in Yorkshire
Highclere Castle is a real country house owned by the Earl and Countess of Carnarvon since 1679
The 5th Earl of Carnarvon co-discovered Tutankhamun's tomb with Howard Carter in 1922
Village scenes filmed in Bampton, Oxfordshire, which became a major tourist destination
Below-stairs scenes (kitchen, servants' hall) were shot on purpose-built sets at Ealing Studios in London
The show's success spawned two sequel films (2019, 2022) and a third (2025), all filmed at Highclere

items.prompt: What is the name of the fictional small town where the Netflix sci-fi series (2016–2025) about kids encountering supernatural forces from the Upside Down is set?
items.explanation:
Fictional small town in Indiana, set in the 1980s
The town's Hawkins National Laboratory conducts secret government experiments that open a portal to the Upside Down
Filmed primarily in and around Atlanta, Georgia—not Indiana
Jackson, Georgia, doubled for downtown Hawkins; other Georgia locations filled in for the surrounding countryside
The Duffer Brothers based the town's atmosphere on 1980s Spielberg and Stephen King small-town settings
Season 4 expanded to California and Russia but Hawkins remained the story's center
The show revived interest in 1980s small-town Americana as a horror and sci-fi setting

items.prompt: What is the name of the fictional small lumber town in the Pacific Northwest where the ABC mystery series (1990–1991) created by David Lynch and Mark Frost is set?
items.explanation:
Fictional lumber town in Washington state, population 51,201
Created by David Lynch and Mark Frost; the show opens with the murder of homecoming queen Laura Palmer
Filmed on location in Snoqualmie, North Bend, and Fall City, Washington—about 30 minutes east of Seattle
Snoqualmie Falls appears in the iconic opening credits; the Salish Lodge doubled as the Great Northern Hotel
Twede's Cafe in North Bend served as the Double R Diner, famous for its cherry pie and "damn fine coffee"
Revived as Twin Peaks: The Return on Showtime in 2017 after a 25-year hiatus, also filmed in the Snoqualmie area
Pioneered the fusion of art-house surrealism with network television; influenced Lost, True Detective, and Fargo

items.prompt: In what northeastern Pennsylvania city is the NBC mockumentary sitcom (2005–2013) set at a paper company's regional branch?
items.explanation:
Set at the Scranton branch of Dunder Mifflin Paper Company
Filmed entirely at Chandler Valley Center Studios in Van Nuys, California—never in Scranton
The show used real Scranton landmarks in dialogue and establishing shots: Steamtown Mall, Lake Wallenpaupack, Poor Richard's bar
Scranton embraced the show: the city installed a "Dunder Mifflin" sign on the Scranton Welcome sign, and a mural of Steve Carell is downtown
Adapted from the BBC series by Ricky Gervais, which was set in Slough, England—another unglamorous office town
The choice of Scranton (a declining former coal city) reinforced the show's themes of mundane work life and quiet desperation
Became the most-streamed show on Netflix before moving to Peacock in 2021

items.prompt: What is the name of the fictional midwestern town where the NBC comedy (2009–2015) about an enthusiastic local government employee in the parks department is set?
items.explanation:
Fictional town in Indiana; its map is actually Muncie, Indiana, flipped upside down
Created by Greg Daniels and Michael Schur as a spiritual companion to The Office
Pawnee's rival city is Eagleton, a wealthy neighboring town separated by a fence
The show built an elaborate fictional history for Pawnee: racist murals in city hall, a raccoon problem, a Li'l Sebastian memorial
Modeled on the city governments of Muncie and Bloomington, Indiana
Filmed in Los Angeles, primarily at CBS Studio Center—the same lot where Seinfeld was made
The fictional Pawnee motto: "First in Friendship, Fourth in Obesity"

items.prompt: In what Appalachian county in eastern Kentucky is the FX crime drama (2010–2015) about a U.S. Marshal sent back to his home region primarily set?
items.explanation:
Set in Harlan County in the coalfields of eastern Kentucky
Based on the writing of Elmore Leonard, particularly his short story "Fire in the Hole"
Timothy Olyphant starred as Deputy U.S. Marshal Raylan Givens, sent back to his Kentucky roots from Miami
Despite the setting, the show was never filmed in Harlan County—the pilot shot in Pittsburgh, PA, and subsequent seasons in California
Kittanning, Pennsylvania, doubled for downtown Harlan; Green Valley, California, stood in for rural Kentucky
Harlan County's real history of coal wars, poverty, and clan feuds informed the show's themes of generational violence
Revived as Justified: City Primeval (2023) on FX, set in Detroit rather than Kentucky

items.prompt: In what southeastern Florida city is the NBC sitcom (1985–1992) about four older women sharing a house set?
items.explanation:
Set at the fictional address 6151 Richmond Street in Miami, Florida
The house belongs to Blanche Devereaux (Rue McClanahan), who rents rooms to Dorothy, Rose, and Sophia
The real exterior was a house at 245 North Saltair Avenue in the Brentwood neighborhood of Los Angeles
All interior scenes filmed on a Hollywood soundstage—the show never filmed in Florida
The Miami setting gave the show its distinctive pastel, tropical aesthetic and justified the lanai (covered patio) as a gathering spot
Ran 7 seasons and 180 episodes; all four leads won Emmy Awards for their roles
The show challenged ageism in television by centering women in their 50s–80s and addressing issues like dating, illness, and mortality

items.prompt: In what major Pacific Northwest city is the ABC medical drama (2005–present) created by Shonda Rhimes, set at a fictional teaching hospital, primarily located?
items.explanation:
Set at the fictional Grey Sloan Memorial Hospital (originally Seattle Grace Hospital) in Seattle
Meredith Grey's "intern house" is a real home at 303 West Comstock Street in the Queen Anne neighborhood of Seattle
Fisher Plaza in Seattle served as the hospital exterior in some shots; the building is actually a broadcast facility
Mostly filmed on soundstages in Los Angeles; fewer Seattle location shoots as the show progressed
The Seattle setting provides recurring visual motifs: ferryboats, the Space Needle skyline, overcast skies, coffee culture
Longest-running primetime medical drama in U.S. television history
Created by Shonda Rhimes, whose production company Shondaland also produced Scandal and How to Get Away with Murder

items.prompt: What Ohio city appears in the title of the CBS sitcom (1978–1982) about the staff of a struggling AM radio station?
items.explanation:
Set at the fictional WKRP, a failing AM radio station in Cincinnati, Ohio
Filmed at MTM Studios in Studio City, California—not in Cincinnati
Establishing shots used real Cincinnati landmarks: Fountain Square, the Roebling Suspension Bridge, the Brent Spence Bridge
The exterior building was the old Cincinnati Enquirer building at 617 Vine Street (now a Hampton Inn)
The "Turkey Drop" Thanksgiving episode is one of the most famous sitcom episodes ever—"As God is my witness, I thought turkeys could fly"
Only one episode was actually filmed in Cincinnati, featuring Kings Island amusement park
The show struggled with music licensing for reruns because it used real rock and pop songs, limiting syndication for decades
